title: resedaceae
intro: The Resedaceae rank is a family of flowering plants, also known as the mignonette or wedgewood family. This family comprises around 40 species of annuals and perennials, commonly found in dry and rocky areas, with distinctive flowers and leaves. The Resedaceae family is native to the Northern Hemisphere, with a majority of species found in Europe and Asia. Notable members of this family include the mignonette (Reseda luteola) and the prickly mignonette (Reseda muricata).
